cases.title: Testing-plan §3 Attempt/audit coverage row requires 100% attendance mutation audit completeness
preconditions: Testing-plan §3 Attempt/audit coverage block applies (FR-22, FR-29, FR-30, BR-22, BR-23, AC-18, AC-19).
dispute-trail fixture provides lecturer correction, admin override, and close-time absent assignments.
M08 Audit and Compliance module persists audit_logs with real database.
steps: Enumerate all committed attendance status mutations in the fixture (manual create/update and system close assignments).
Query audit_logs for each mutation by targetType AttendanceRecord, targetId, and occurredAt window.
Verify each audit row includes actorUserId, timestamp, target student and session, oldValue/oldStatus, newValue/newStatus, and reason where mandated.
Compare mutation count to audit row count for the same correlation scope.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan §3.1 Attempt/audit coverage: every committed attendance mutation has exactly one queryable audit record with full CMP-01 fields per FR-29 and BR-22; zero mutation-audit gaps.

cases.title: M08 writeAuditEvent persists lecturer manual correction audit row in same transaction as M05 ledger update
preconditions: Real database transaction spans M05 Attendance Ledger and M08 Audit and Compliance modules.
Closed session exists with enrolled student AttendanceRecord status Absent.
Assigned Lecturer is within manualEditWindow per M06 policy resolution.
Policy mandates reason for manual corrections.
steps: Capture beforeState: AttendanceRecord status Absent; count audit_logs rows for target studentUserId and classSessionId.
Invoke M05 correctAttendance through application service with status Manual Present, reason, actor Lecturer, and Idempotency-Key.
Query attendance_records and audit_logs tables within the same test transaction scope.
Verify M08 writeAuditEvent payload includes actorUserId, role Lecturer, target student/session, oldStatus Absent, newStatus Manual Present, reason, occurredAt, and correlationId.
Assert audit_logs row is append-only and immutable after commit.
cases.expected: M08 module boundary writes exactly one audit_logs row atomically with attendance mutation: actor, timestamp, target student and session, previous value Absent, new value Manual Present, and reason per FR-29 and BR-22.

cases.title: M08 audit service records system-actor rows for each close-time auto-absent assignment
preconditions: Open session exists with at least three enrolled students in Pending state without successful check-in.
M03 session close processor triggers M05 absent finalization as system process.
Real database with audit_logs append-only constraints.
steps: Execute POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close through session lifecycle service.
Query attendance_records confirming Pending students transitioned to Absent.
Query audit_logs filtered by actionType attendance_status_mutation and classSessionId for close-time events.
Verify each auto-absent student has a distinct audit row with system or close-actor context, oldStatus Pending, newStatus Absent, and occurredAt.
cases.expected: 100% coverage for system-process attendance mutations: each close-time Absent assignment writes one audit_logs row with actor context, timestamp, target student/session, previous Pending, new Absent per FR-29, FR-09, and BR-22 exception for system actor.

cases.title: SM-03 attendance status transitions via admin override each produce auditable beforeState and afterState
preconditions: Integration database seeded with students in Absent, Present, and Late states on a Closed session.
AcademicAdmin actor is available with override authority per BR-16.
Audit query module can filter by targetId and actionType.
steps: AcademicAdmin corrects Absent student to Excused with documented reason; capture beforeState, afterState, and audit row.
AcademicAdmin overrides Late student to Manual Present with reason; capture audit row.
Query audit_logs for both mutations and verify oldStatus/newStatus pairs match captured states.
Confirm checkInMethod Admin Correction is reflected where applicable.
cases.expected: Each legal SM-03 AttendanceRecord transition via admin override commits with a queryable audit record documenting beforeState, afterState, actor AcademicAdmin, reason, and occurredAt per FR-29 and BR-22.

cases.title: Idempotent attendance mutation replay returns prior audit summary without duplicate audit_logs rows
preconditions: Closed session with Absent student exists; assigned Lecturer is within edit window.
Idempotency-Key support is enabled for PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId}.
Real database with audit_logs immutability constraints.
steps: Issue PATCH correction with status Manual Present, reason, and Idempotency-Key K1; record audit row id and count.
Replay identical PATCH with same Idempotency-Key K1, same body, and same actor.
Count audit_logs rows for the student-session pair and compare second response to first.
Verify AttendanceRecord afterState unchanged from first successful mutation.
cases.expected: First committed mutation writes exactly one audit row; idempotent replay returns already-applied summary without a second audit_logs entry, preserving FR-29 100% coverage without duplicate audit records.

cases.title: GET /v1/audit-logs returns 200 with manual correction audit envelope after PATCH attendance
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with bearer token.
Closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent; manual edit window is active.
AcademicAdmin bearer token can query audit logs per role policy.
steps: Lecturer calls PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present, reason, and Idempotency-Key; confirm HTTP 200.
AcademicAdmin calls GET /v1/audit-logs?targetType=AttendanceRecord&targetId={studentUserId}&actionType=manual_update&from={mutationStart}&to={mutationEnd}&page=1&pageSize=25.
Inspect HTTP 200 response envelope, meta.pagination, and matching audit entry payload fields.
Verify error is null and data array contains the committed mutation audit row with oldStatus, newStatus, actorUserId, reason, and occurredAt.
cases.expected: PATCH returns HTTP 200 and committed mutation is queryable via GET /v1/audit-logs with standard envelope; audit entry includes all FR-29 required fields per API §4.7 and CMP-01.

cases.title: POST /v1/auth/login and GET /v1/me establish actor context recorded in attendance mutation audit
preconditions: Lecturer account exists with institution-issued credentials and assigned class section scope.
Closed session with Absent student exists; manual edit window is active.
AcademicAdmin can query GET /v1/audit-logs after mutation.
steps: Call POST /v1/auth/login with lecturer email and password; assert HTTP 200 with accessToken and roles including Lecturer.
Call GET /v1/me with Authorization bearer token; assert HTTP 200 with actor userId and assigned section scope claims.
Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with Manual Present status, reason, and Idempotency-Key using the same bearer token.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for the mutation and verify actorUserId matches GET /v1/me identity.
cases.expected: Authenticated actor from FR-15 and FR-36 is persisted as actorUserId in audit_logs row with role Lecturer, target student/session, old/new status, reason, and timestamp per FR-29.

cases.title: POST /v1/enrollments/import seeds roster then lecturer correction writes audit for imported student
preconditions: AcademicAdmin has created class section with closed session.
POST /v1/enrollments/import accepts enrolled student row for the section.
Session close assigned Absent to imported student without check-in.
Assigned Lecturer is within manualEditWindow.
steps: AcademicAdmin calls POST /v1/enrollments/import with classSectionId and valid studentCode rows; assert HTTP 200 with acceptedRows count and row-level rejection summary when applicable.
Lecturer calls PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present and reason.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs filtered by target studentUserId and classSessionId.
Verify audit row references imported student with oldStatus Absent and newStatus Manual Present.
cases.expected: Enrollment import establishes eligible roster per FR-04; subsequent manual correction audit is written with full FR-29 fields for the imported enrolled student without audit gap.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance roster update correlates with queryable mutation audit entry
preconditions: Open or closed session exists with enrolled student showing Absent on roster.
Assigned Lecturer is authenticated and within manualEditWindow.
Baseline GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance captured before correction.
steps: Lecturer calls PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Late and documented reason.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and assert roster row shows attendanceStatus Late and checkInMethod Manual.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for manual_update action on the same student-session pair.
Verify audit oldStatus matches baseline roster status and newStatus matches updated roster row.
cases.expected: Live roster GET per FR-19 reflects committed mutation; corresponding audit_logs entry is queryable with previous and new values matching roster beforeState and afterState per FR-29.

cases.title: E2E-06 primary workflow lecturer manual correction produces complete audit trail
preconditions: Lecturer opens and closes session for assigned section.
Session close assigns Absent to enrolled student without successful check-in.
Manual edit window from effective policy is active.
Authorized role can query GET /v1/audit-logs.
steps: Lecturer reviews GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance showing student Absent.
Lecturer submits PATCH correction to Manual Present with documented reason.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs filtered by classSessionId and studentUserId for manual_update action.
Verify audit fields against permissions §7.1 minimum audit fields for manual attendance update.
cases.expected: End-to-end E2E-06 path writes queryable audit record with actor, role Lecturer, target student/session, old status Absent, new status Manual Present, reason, and timestamp per FR-29 and CMP-01.

cases.title: Flow C audit verification after AcademicAdmin escalated attendance override mutation
preconditions: Closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent after lecturer edit window expired.
AcademicAdmin is authenticated with override authority per BR-16.
SystemAuditor read scope can query audit logs.
steps: Record roster beforeState via GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance.
AcademicAdmin applies PATCH correction with status Excused and documented override reason.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs with filters targetType AttendanceRecord, targetId, actionType admin_override, and time range.
Verify audit payload includes actor AcademicAdmin, old status, new status Excused, reason, approval context, and occurredAt.
cases.expected: Committed admin override mutation has corresponding queryable audit record with full FR-29 fields; no mutation-audit gap per BR-22 and CMP-01.

cases.title: Alternate branch session close finalization writes audit row per auto-absent mutation
preconditions: Open session has at least five enrolled students with mixed Present and Pending states.
Lecturer bearer token is available for session close.
AcademicAdmin can query audit logs institution-scoped.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close and records closedAt.
Query GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance confirming Pending students became Absent.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs?targetType=AttendanceRecord&classSessionId={sessionId}&actionType=status_finalization&from={closedAt}&page=1&pageSize=100.
Count audit entries against number of students whose status mutated at close and verify each includes oldStatus Pending and newStatus Absent.
cases.expected: Each system-process absent assignment at close has a queryable audit record; audit count matches committed mutation count with full FR-29 field set per BR-22.

cases.title: GET /v1/audit-logs pagination returns scoped attendance mutation entries across page boundaries
preconditions: dispute-trail fixture includes at least 30 committed attendance status mutation audit entries for one class section.
AcademicAdmin bearer token is authenticated within institution scope.
Mutations span lecturer corrections and close-time absent assignments with full CMP-01 fields.
steps: Query GET /v1/audit-logs?targetType=AttendanceRecord&classSectionId={sectionId}&from={rangeStart}&to={rangeEnd}&page=1&pageSize=25.
Verify HTTP 200, meta.pagination page=1, pageSize=25, totalItems >= 30, and totalPages >= 2.
Record audit entry IDs from page 1 and verify each row includes actor, oldStatus, newStatus, reason where required, and occurredAt.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs with same filters and page=2; verify distinct IDs with no overlap.
cases.expected: Paginated audit query honors page and pageSize invariants; all FR-29 mutation audit entries remain queryable across pages with complete actor, timestamp, target, previous value, new value, and reason fields.

cases.title: Rejected PATCH correction outside edit window writes no attendance mutation audit row
preconditions: Closed session with Absent student exists; manual edit window has expired for Lecturer.
Lecturer bearer token is authenticated for assigned section.
Audit log baseline count for the student-session pair is captured before test.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present and reason as Lecturer.
Confirm HTTP 409 EditWindowExpired; AttendanceRecord remains Absent.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs and audit_logs table for new manual_update rows tied to the rejected request.
Verify no committed mutation audit exists documenting old/new status change.
cases.expected: Rejected correction returns documented error without AttendanceRecord mutation; no audit_logs row documents a successful status change for the failed attempt; FR-29 applies only when mutation is committed.

cases.title: Student and out-of-scope roles denied audit log read without leaking mutation details
preconditions: Committed attendance mutation audit exists for session in section A with known target student.
Student bearer token, Lecturer token for unassigned section B, and ITAdmin token without academic grant are available.
AcademicAdmin token is available as authorized control for audit query.
steps: Student calls GET /v1/audit-logs?targetId={studentUserId}&classSessionId={sessionIdA}.
Out-of-scope Lecturer (section B) calls GET /v1/audit-logs filtered to section A mutation events.
ITAdmin calls GET /v1/audit-logs for academic attendance mutation without explicit academic grant.
Inspect responses for partial leakage of oldStatus, newStatus, actor, or reason fields.
AcademicAdmin calls the same audit query as control.
cases.expected: Student receives HTTP 403 Forbidden per AuditLog.read denial; out-of-scope Lecturer receives HTTP 403 OutOfScope; ITAdmin receives technical-scope-only or 403 without academic mutation payload; AcademicAdmin control returns HTTP 200 with full FR-29 audit fields per BR-19.

cases.title: System Auditor PG-15 audit log review displays attendance mutation entry with old/new status columns
preconditions: Committed lecturer manual correction audit exists for closed session with known student.
SystemAuditor is authenticated on desktop browser with read-only audit scope.
PG-15 Audit log review route /audit/logs is available per FLOW-13.
steps: Navigate to /audit/logs and apply TableToolbar filters: targetType AttendanceRecord, actionType manual_update, and date range covering the mutation.
Locate AuditEntryRow for the committed correction in the paginated results table.
Verify row displays actor name, timestamp, target student/session identifiers, old status Absent, new status Manual Present, and reason text.
Confirm pagination controls and default sort timestamp desc per PG-15 listing spec.
cases.expected: Browser FLOW-13 audit review journey displays queryable attendance mutation audit with all FR-29 required fields visible in AuditEntryRow per CMP-01; authorized SystemAuditor can investigate dispute trail without mutation privileges.
